name: Whole Video
description: The video analyzed as a single atomic unit — one row of data.
finding: Keep Rate (r=0.43) and Retention % (r=0.27) are strongest predictors at this resolution. Net Novelty +2 sweet spot. Zeigarnik Z=6-7 sweet spot.

name: Hook vs Body
description: Video split into two segments: Hook (opening, analyzed) and Body (rest). First step above whole-video resolution.
finding: Zeigarnik Type B (Challenge/outcome uncertainty) dominates — 88 videos, avg 9.6M views. Hooks scored via LLM on opening transcript.

name: Second-by-Second Retention
description: Retention curve analyzed per second across video length. Identifies exact drop-off moments.
finding: Data available (retentionCurve in analysis.json for 372 videos). Not yet analyzed — next experiment is identifying common drop-off second pattern across viral vs non-viral videos.

name: First 3 Seconds
description: The critical opening micro-segment scored via LLM on hook text + transcript opening.
finding: Currently scored via LLM on hook description + opening transcript lines. Visual and audio dimensions not yet captured. LLM scoring is a proxy — needs frame-level validation.

name: First Frame
description: Single frame — what the viewer sees before deciding to watch. For Shorts, this is the auto-playing moment.
finding: Not yet analyzed. Frame images exist in video_data/[ytId]/frames/. Next: LLM visual analysis for face presence, color saturation, visual complexity, motion suggestion, text overlay.

name: Audio Layer (First 3s)
description: Isolated audio analysis of the opening 3 seconds — music, voice, SFX, silence.
finding: Not yet analyzed. Would require audio extraction from video files.
